question_title: Will the interest in “laura loomer” change between 2026-03-18 and 2026-03-27 according to Google Trends?
summary:
Laura Loomer's Google Trends interest spiked to 100 on Mar 15 due to her controversial appearance at the India Today Conclave in New Delhi, where journalist Rajdeep Sardesai confronted her with deleted anti-Indian tweets. By Mar 18, the value had decayed to 61 — still well above the pre-spike baseline of ~25. The question asks whether the Mar 27 value will be higher, lower, or within ±3 of the Mar 18 value (61).

The dominant outcome is Decreases. The starting value of 61 is 2.4x the pre-spike baseline, and classic post-spike decay dynamics strongly favor return toward baseline over a 9-day horizon. Even under slow-decay scenarios (daily decay rate 0.92), the projected Mar 27 value (~50) falls well below the 58 threshold for Decreases. Monte Carlo simulations across multiple decay assumptions consistently produce ~85% probability of Decreases, with median end values in the 30-50 range. The narrow [58, 64] window for "Doesn't change" makes that outcome improbable given the high volatility (25.5). "Increases" (>64) would require a new major spike/catalyst, which is possible given Loomer's propensity for controversy but not the base case.

factors.description: Post-spike decay dynamics: Value spiked to 100 on Mar 15 (India Today Conclave), decayed to 61 by Mar 18. Still well above pre-spike baseline of ~25. Classic post-spike decay pattern over 9 days strongly favors returning toward baseline, meaning Decreases.
Pre-spike baseline of ~25 is far below the 58 threshold. Even slow decay over 9 days from 61 toward 25 will likely cross below 58 well before Mar 27.
Monte Carlo simulations across multiple decay scenarios consistently show Decreases as dominant outcome (85% across weighted scenarios), with median end values in 30-50 range.
Threshold arithmetic: 'Doesn't change' requires end value in [58,64] — a narrow 7-point window. Given trailing volatility of 25.5, landing in this narrow band is unlikely.
Ongoing news coverage: CAIR legal case ruling, Pentagon press pass, Trump connections, and India trip aftermath provide continued story lines that could sustain or re-spike interest.
Decay stalled at 61 (same value Mar 17 and Mar 18), suggesting the story might have longer legs than typical news spikes.

reasoning:
**Forecast Summary:**

| Outcome | Probability |
|---------|-------------|
| **Decreases** | **83%** |
| Increases | 10% |
| Doesn't change | 7% |

**Key reasoning:** Laura Loomer's search interest spiked to 100 on Mar 15 due to the India Today Conclave controversy (journalist reading her deleted anti-Indian tweets back to her on stage). By Mar 18, interest had decayed to 61 — still 2.4× the pre-spike baseline of ~25. Over the next 9 days to Mar 27, standard post-spike decay strongly favors the value dropping below 58 (the "Decreases" threshold). Monte Carlo simulations across multiple decay assumptions consistently produce ~85% probability of Decreases, with projected median end values in the 30–50 range. The narrow 7-point window [58, 64] for "Doesn't change" and the need for a new major catalyst for "Increases" (>64) make those outcomes less likely.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=laura%20loomer&date=2026-02-25%202026-03-27](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=laura%20loomer&date=2026-02-25%202026-03-27) for 2026-03-27 is within 3 of the value at 2026-03-18. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "laura loomer",
  "date": "2026-02-25 2026-03-27",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
